name: paypal_payment_integrity
description: Canonical business-integrity rules for PayPal Payments API v2 validation. Covers all 9 Axiom rule types. Derived from PayPal API contract, PCI-DSS v4, and internal settlement-reconciliation policy.

rules.description: Request timestamp — changes on every call; non-semantic for behavioral comparison.
Capture timestamp — non-semantic; suppressed to avoid false-positive drift.
PayPal processing fee MUST be returned on every payment response. Merchants use this field for net-settlement reconciliation. Absence → revenue leakage invisible to downstream accounting systems.
ISO 4217 currency code is mandatory on all monetary responses. SWIFT and forex routing systems are case-sensitive and reject malformed codes.
Internal error codes must never appear in payment API responses. PCI-DSS forbids exposing internal system states to external callers.
Payment FSM state must be a documented value. Undocumented states (e.g. PROCESSING) break downstream payment processors, PSPs, and webhook consumers.
Currency must be uppercase ISO 4217. Lowercase codes (e.g. 'usd') are rejected by SWIFT messaging and forex settlement systems — silent routing failure.
fraud_score above 0.35 triggers mandatory manual review. A silent pass-through above threshold is a PCI-DSS v4 §10.7 compliance breach. Scores above 0.8 indicate full model failure.
Low-risk transactions must carry the literal APPROVE decision. Any other value (REVIEW, DECLINE) for a clean score triggers an unwarranted friction path, blocking legitimate checkouts.
Monetary amounts must be reproducible to sub-cent precision. Drift beyond 0.001 indicates a floating-point or rounding regression — breaks PCI-DSS auditability and reconciliation.
A COMPLETED payment must always carry a positive net_amount. Absence or zero value on a COMPLETED record means settlement records are corrupt — regulatory reporting failure.
